message.content:
import { Map, Swords, Trophy, Target, Shield, Sparkles } from 'lucide-react'
import { Callout } from '@/components/mdx/Callout'
import { Card, CardGrid } from '@/components/mdx/CardGrid'
import { Checklist } from '@/components/mdx/Checklist'
import { FAQ } from '@/components/mdx/FAQ'
import { Steps, Step } from '@/components/mdx/Steps'
import { Section } from '@/components/mdx/Section'
import { YouTubeEmbed } from '@/components/mdx/YouTubeEmbed'

export const metadata = {
  title: "Bizarre Lineage Kendo: Master the Sword Style & Dominate in 2026",
  description: "Unleash the power of Kendo in Bizarre Lineage! Discover how to get the Kendo fighting style, its unique moves, and pro tips for dominating opponents in 2026.",
  category: "Guide",
  image: "https://placehold.co/800x400/8B4513/fff?text=Bizarre+Lineage+Kendo",
  date: "2026-03-04",
  author: "Bizarre Lineage Wiki Team"
}

<Callout type="info" title="Quick Guide">
- The **Bizarre Lineage Kendo** fighting style offers high skill ceiling combat with guard-breaking potential.
- Unlock Kendo by completing the "Warrior's Pilgrimage" questline at **Bus Stop 15** in Morioh Cho.
- Key Kendo moves like **Whirlwind** and **Rising Tempest** are crucial for combos and control.
- Kendo pairs exceptionally well with **Anubis** for weapon-scaling builds, enhancing overall damage.
- Be prepared to pay **$100** to the Kendo Sword Master to initiate the questline.
</Callout>

Embarking on your journey in Bizarre Lineage means choosing a fighting style that resonates with your combat preferences. Among the various options, **Bizarre Lineage Kendo** stands out as a unique and powerful choice for players who favor precision, swift strikes, and strategic engagement. This guide will walk you through everything you need to know about acquiring the Kendo fighting style, mastering its intricate moveset, and integrating it into a formidable build to dominate your opponents in 2026. Whether you're a seasoned player looking for a new challenge or a newcomer seeking the ultimate sword-fighting experience, Kendo offers a rewarding path to mastery.

<Section icon={Map} title="Unlocking the Path of the Sword: Getting Kendo in Bizarre Lineage">
The Kendo fighting style in Bizarre Lineage is a formidable choice for those who appreciate disciplined sword combat. To begin your journey as a Kendo master, you'll need to locate the Kendo Sword Master NPC and complete his challenging questline. This style is known for its high skill ceiling and unique guard-breaking capabilities, making it a valuable asset in both PvP and PvE encounters.

**Video Highlights:**
- Kendo trainer location at Bus Stop 5 (or 15 in some sources).
- Overview of Kendo's three main abilities: Whirlwind, Silent Edge, and Rising Tempest.
- Demonstration of Kendo's heavy attack and combo potential.
- Suggestion to pair Kendo with Anubis for optimal synergy.

<YouTubeEmbed videoId="0rkwFx8FjLw" title="Bizarre Lineage How To Get (Kendo,Boxing,Karate) Fighting Style Fast Location + Full Guide!" />

<Callout type="tip" title="Pro Tip">
Before committing to a fighting style, ensure you have a solid Stand and have leveled your core stats. Fighting Styles, especially Kendo, are most effective when paired with a Sub-Ability (Spec) to create a complete and synergistic combat build.
</Callout>

To acquire the **Bizarre Lineage Kendo** fighting style, you must undertake the "Warrior's Pilgrimage" questline. This quest involves several steps that test your combat prowess and dedication. The Kendo Sword Master will guide you through a series of tasks, culminating in you earning the full Kendo moveset.

<Steps>
  <Step num="1" title="Locate the Kendo Sword Master">
    Fast travel to **Bus Stop 15**. Turn around and enter the nearby **Kame Yu Market**. You'll find the Kendo Sword Master NPC in the middle of the building. Talk to him and pay **$100** to accept the "Warrior's Pilgrimage" quest.
  </Step>
  <Step num="2" title="Kendo Quest 1: Bandit Beatdown">
    Your first task is to defeat nearby Samurai. These can be found both inside and outside the Kame Yu Market. Complete this to earn your first Kendo move.
  </Step>
  <Step num="3" title="Kendo Quest 2: Inner Reflection">
    Follow the quest marker to a meditation spot. Here, you'll engage in combat with yourself 3 times, pushing your mental and physical limits.
  </Step>
  <Step num="4" title="Kendo Quest 3: Master of the Blade">
    Return to the Kame Yu Market and defeat **70 Samurai Masters**. This rigorous training will unlock your second Kendo move.
  </Step>
  <Step num="5" title="Kendo Quest 4: World Event & Duels">
    Complete a World Event, which typically spawns at Bus Station 1 every 30 minutes. Additionally, you must win **3 1v1 duels**. You can ask other players to trade kills at the PVP board for an easier completion.
  </Step>
  <Step num="6" title="Kendo Quest 6: Final Lesson">
    Return to the Kendo Sword Master NPC one last time. Upon successful completion of all tasks, he will bestow upon you the third and final Kendo move, completing your Kendo training.
  </Step>
</Steps>
</Section>

<Section icon={Swords} title="Kendo Mastery: Moveset & Abilities">
The **Bizarre Lineage Kendo** fighting style is characterized by its precise slashes, agile movements, and powerful combo potential. Mastering its moveset is key to becoming a dominant force on the battlefield. Each ability serves a specific purpose, from initiating combat to delivering devastating finishing blows.

| Kendo Move       | Keybind | Description                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             |
| :--------------- | :------ | :---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **Whirlwind**    | [E]     | After a small windup, perform a swift slash in front of you, hitting everyone in a small arc. This move is crucial for **breaking the guard** of defensive opponents, creating openings for follow-up attacks.                                                              |
| **Silent Edge**  | [R]     | Dash forward with your sword before executing a powerful slash. If successful, you can chain multiple slashes consecutively until the enemy is stunned. It's a highly reliable **gap-closer and combo starter**.                                                           |
| **Rising Tempest** | [F]     | Stomp the ground, launching enemies upwards if hit. You then perform 3 aerial slashes, followed by a final strike that sends you and the enemy falling. You slide across the floor, dragging the enemy beneath you for additional damage before knocking them out. Offers a **high damage ceiling** with impressive visual flair. |

<Callout type="info">
Kendo's unique **guard-breaking** capability with Whirlwind sets it apart. This allows Kendo users to bypass enemy defenses, making it a potent style against tanky or defensive builds. Practice timing this move to maximize its effectiveness.
</Callout>

Beyond the special abilities, equipping Kendo also transforms your basic M1 and M2 attacks, changing their animations and contributing to your overall combat identity. This integration ensures that Kendo feels like a complete and immersive fighting style.
</Section>

<Section icon={Target} title="Kendo vs. The Competition: Comparing Fighting Styles">
While **Bizarre Lineage Kendo** excels in precise, guard-breaking swordplay, it's helpful to understand how it stacks up against other available fighting styles like Boxing and Karate. Each style offers a distinct playstyle, catering to different combat preferences and strategies.

<CardGrid cols={3}>
  <Card title="Kendo: The Master Swordsman">
    - **High Skill Ceiling**: Rewards precision and timing.
    - **Guard Break**: Essential for bypassing defenses.
    - **Combo Potential**: Excellent for chaining attacks.
    - Pairs well with **weapon-scaling Stands**.
  </Card>
  <Card title="Boxing: The Agile Brawler">
    - **Beginner-Friendly**: Straightforward moveset.
    - **Fast Stuns**: Reliable crowd control.
    - **Reactive Counter**: Dempsey Roll punishes aggression.
    - Ideal for **PvP** and interrupting opponents.
  </Card>
  <Card title="Karate: The Mobile Striker">
    - **Mobile AoE**: Great for group fights.
    - **Aerial Moves**: Offers unique movement and attack angles.
    - **Multi-hit**: Effective for damaging multiple targets.
    - Strong choice for **PvE** and crowd control.
  </Card>
</CardGrid>

To further illustrate the differences, here's a breakdown of each style's recommended playstyle, strengths, and ideal scenarios:

| Fighting Style | Playstyle        | Strengths                                  | Best For                                   |
| :------------- | :--------------- | :----------------------------------------- | :----------------------------------------- |
| **Kendo**      | Precise Striker  | Guard break, combo potential, high damage  | Experienced players, PvP, weapon builds    |
| **Boxing**     | Brawler          | Fast stuns, easy execution, reactive counter | Beginners, PvP, interrupting combos        |
| **Karate**     | Mobile AoE       | Aerial moves, multi-hit, area damage       | Group fights, PvE, mobility                |

<Callout type="success" title="Synergy Spotlight">
For players utilizing weapon-based Stands, **Bizarre Lineage Kendo** offers exceptional synergy. Specifically, combining Kendo with **Anubis** is highly recommended, as both scale with the weapon stat. This combination significantly amplifies your overall damage output and combat effectiveness.
</Callout>
</Section>

<Section icon={Sparkles} title="Advanced Kendo Tactics & Combos">
Mastering **Bizarre Lineage Kendo** goes beyond just knowing the moves; it involves understanding how to chain them effectively and adapt to various combat situations. Kendo's strengths lie in its ability to open up defensive opponents and maintain pressure with relentless combos.

Here are some key strategies and practice items for advanced Kendo players:

<Checklist
  id="kendo-mastery"
  title="Kendo Mastery Checklist:"
  items={[
    "Practice **Whirlwind** timing for consistent guard breaks.",
    "Learn to seamlessly chain **Silent Edge** into multiple slashes.",
    "Master the **Rising Tempest** combo for maximum damage output.",
    "Experiment with different Stand abilities to find optimal Kendo synergies.",
    "Utilize Kendo's M1/M2 attack animations to extend combos and maintain pressure."
  ]}
/>

A fundamental Kendo combo often starts with **Whirlwind** to break an opponent's guard, followed by **Silent Edge** to close the gap and initiate a multi-hit sequence. You can then finish with **Rising Tempest** for high burst damage and crowd control. The key is to practice these transitions until they become muscle memory.

<Callout type="warning" title="Kendo's Learning Curve">
While powerful, Kendo has a higher skill ceiling compared to styles like Boxing. It requires precise execution and good situational awareness to maximize its potential. Don't get discouraged if it takes time to master; the rewards are well worth the effort.
</Callout>

Remember that your fighting style is a core part of your overall combat identity, and Kendo's dynamic animations and guard-breaking utility make it a standout choice for those who enjoy active and engaging melee combat.
</Section>

<Section icon={Trophy} title="Bizarre Lineage Kendo: Frequently Asked Questions">
We've compiled answers to some of the most common questions about the Kendo fighting style in Bizarre Lineage to help you on your journey.

<FAQ items={[
  {
    question: "What is the best fighting style in Bizarre Lineage?",
    answer: "There isn't a single 'best' style, as it depends on your playstyle. **Bizarre Lineage Kendo** is excellent for experienced players due to its guard-breaking and combo potential. Boxing is beginner-friendly with reliable stuns, while Karate excels in PvE and group fights with its AoE moves."
  },
  {
    question: "Do you need money to learn the Kendo fighting style in Bizarre Lineage?",
    answer: "Yes, you need to pay **$100** to the Kendo Sword Master NPC to begin the 'Warrior's Pilgrimage' questline. Ensure you have the funds before attempting to unlock Kendo."
  },
  {
    question: "How many fighting styles can you equip at once?",
    answer: "You can only equip one fighting style at a time in Bizarre Lineage. However, once you've learned multiple styles, you can switch between them freely from your build menu."
  },
  {
    question: "Does Kendo change your M1 and M2 attacks?",
    answer: "Yes, equipping the Kendo fighting style in Bizarre Lineage changes the animations of your basic M1 and M2 attacks, not just your special moves. This makes Kendo a fundamental part of your combat identity."
  },
  {
    question: "Where is the Kendo trainer located?",
    answer: "The Kendo Sword Master NPC is located in the **Kame Yu Market**, near **Bus Stop 15**. Fast travel to Bus Stop 15 and look for the market building."
  },
  {
    question: "Can Kendo be combined with Stands?",
    answer: "Absolutely! Fighting styles are designed to complement Stands. Kendo, in particular, pairs exceptionally well with **Anubis** due to both scaling with the weapon stat, creating a highly effective and damaging build."
  }
]} />
</Section>
